Middle East Banks  
These banks have completed the integration and started injecting trial liquidity.  

_Saudi Arabia  
Al Rajhi Bank and the National Bank of Saudi Arabia (SNB).  
Status: Node synchronization active.  
Linking wallets to the developed payment system.  

_United Arab Emirates  
First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB)  
and Emirates NBD (ENBD)  
Activating the digital dirham as a bridge for payments.  

_Egypt  
Central Bank of Egypt  
(via the new digital currency gateway)  
and Bank of Egypt.  

Status: Cross-border transaction security check.  

_Qatar  
Qatar National Bank (QNB)  
Injecting and preparing liquidity allocated for the travel and aviation services sector.  

#PiNetwork
post_highlights: _Egypt Central Bank of Egypt (via the new digital currency gateway) and Bank of Egypt. • _Saudi Arabia Al Rajhi Bank and the National Bank of Saudi Arabia (SNB). • _United Arab Emirates First Abu Dhabi Bank (FAB) and Emirates NBD (ENBD) Activating the digital dirham as a bridge for payments.

html: **UAE commits $5 billion in fresh investments into Bharat.** Breakdown: - $1 billion by ADIA with NIIF for priority infrastructure - $3 billion by Emirates NBD in RBL Bank - $1 billion by International Holding Company in Sammaan Capital Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri rightly called PM Modi’s UAE visit “substantive”. This capital inflow is a strong vote of confidence in India’s growth story, strengthening our banking sector, infrastructure push and overall economic resilience. This is exactly how strategic partnerships deliver — real money, real projects, and deeper trust. Bharat is steadily attracting serious global capital on its own terms. #BharatNiti #StrategicIndia
text_1:
**UAE commits $5 billion in fresh investments into Bharat.**

Breakdown:  
- $1 billion by ADIA with NIIF for priority infrastructure  
- $3 billion by Emirates NBD in RBL Bank  
- $1 billion by International Holding Company in Sammaan Capital  

Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri rightly called PM Modi’s UAE visit “substantive”. This capital inflow is a strong vote of confidence in India’s growth story, strengthening our banking sector, infrastructure push and overall economic resilience.

This is exactly how strategic partnerships deliver — real money, real projects, and deeper trust. Bharat is steadily attracting serious global capital on its own terms.

#BharatNiti #StrategicIndia

text:
RBL Bank Secures $3B Investment from Emirates NBD 🏦💼 | MCap 20,197.23 Cr

- RBL Bank receives regulatory approval for a $3B (~₹26,850 crore) investment from Emirates NBD.
- Emirates NBD will subscribe to 959M+ shares at ₹280 per share, acquiring 51%-74% stake post-transaction.
- Deal includes merging Emirates NBD's India branches (Mumbai, Chennai, Gurugram) into RBL Bank, pending approvals.
- Investment aims to boost RBL Bank's growth, tech capabilities, and international opportunities.
- RBL Bank leadership highlights the deal's transformative potential for the bank's future.

text:
How did the elephants know?

Two days after a South African conservationist named Lawrence Anthony passed of a heart attack in 2012, two herds of wild elephants walked for twelve hours through the bush to reach his house at the Thula Thula reserve in eastern South Africa. Twenty-one animals in total, who had not been to the house in over a year, arriving on their own without anyone calling them or leading them.

Lawrence was the man who’d saved them. Years earlier the herd had been marked for shooting after escaping multiple enclosures and rampaging through populated land. He took them in when no one else would, camped near them for weeks, talked to them, sang to them, slowly earning the trust of the matriarch, Nana. They had lived peacefully on his reserve ever since.

They stood at his property for two days, making low rumbling calls, restless, ears flaring. Then they turned and walked back into the bush.

The next year, on the anniversary of his passing, they came back. And the year after that. And the year after that.

Nobody can fully explain it. Elephants communicate over long distances using infrasound, low-frequency rumbles that travel for miles below the range of human hearing. They have the largest brain of any land animal, with a memory and a capacity for grief that researchers are still trying to measure. They mourn their own dead, sometimes returning to bones years later and gently touching them. Whether what happened at Thula Thula was a herd somehow sensing the loss of a man they’d bonded with, or a coincidence reframed by grief, is something even the people who were there have stopped trying to settle.

Lawrence’s wife Françoise, who was at the house when the herd arrived, has said the simplest thing about it. “Some things in this world cannot be explained by reason. Cannot be seen.”

text: Emirates NBD announces that, with the receipt of the approval of the Government of India, all requisite regulatory and governmental approvals for Emirates NBD’s landmark acquisition of a majority controlling stake in RBL Bank Limited (RBL) have been received. This milestone clears the path for the imminent completion of a transaction that represents one of the most significant cross-border investment in India’s financial services sector.
html: Ahmed bin Saeed, Chairman of Emirates NBD: We extend our appreciation and gratitude to the wise and visionary leadership of India and the UAE who have enabled the completion of this remarkable milestone. We are confident in DP World’s ability to overcome challenges and continue driving growth and innovation through its expertise and global partnerships.

📊 RBL BANK LTD - 540065 | 🪒 Press Release / Media Release

RBL Bank has received all regulatory and governmental approvals for a strategic investment by Emirates NBD, amounting to approximately ₹24900.00 Cr (USD 3.00 Bn) (~₹ 26,850 crore), marking a significant milestone in the Indian banking sector.

Key Highlights:
Investment Details:
- Emirates NBD will subscribe to up to 959,045,636 fully paid equity shares of RBL Bank at ₹280 per share.
- 

This investment represents approximately 60% of RBL Bank’s post-issue paid-up share capital.

Ownership Structure:
- Emirates NBD’s final shareholding is expected to range between 51% and 74% of total paid-up share capital.
- Upon completion, Emirates NBD will be recognized as the promoter of RBL Bank.

Strategic Impact:
- The investment is expected to unlock new international opportunities and accelerate expansion across high-growth segments.
- RBL Bank aims to enhance its ability to serve a more global and diversified customer base.

Regulatory Compliance:
- The approval process aligns with the investment agreement dated 18 October 2025.
- RBL Bank will operate as a foreign bank subsidiary in accordance with the Reserve Bank of India’s framework.

Mr. Vishwavir Ahuja, Managing Director & CEO, stated, "The approval reinforces confidence in our franchise and positions us to unlock new growth opportunities."

This strategic investment positions RBL Bank strongly for its next phase of growth, focusing on technology, risk management, and customer experience.

💰 CMP: ₹338.00 (+0.31%)
🏢 MCap: ₹20,900 Cr

🌐Details: tinyurl.com/2ae75trn

text:
RBL Bank Announces Receipt of All Regulatory & Governmental Approvals for Strategic Investment by Emirates NBD 🏦

Mumbai, 15 May 2026: RBL Bank Limited (RBL Bank) has achieved a major milestone by successfully completing the regulatory & governmental approval process for the proposed strategic investment by Emirates NBD. This significant development, first announced on October 18, 2025, marks a crucial step towards the transaction's finalization. 🚀

Key Highlights:

- 𝗜𝗻𝘃𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗔𝗺𝗼𝘂𝗻𝘁: Emirates NBD will make a primary infusion of approximately USD 3 billion (~₹26,850 crore), one of the largest international investments in India's banking sector. 💰
- 𝗚𝗿𝗼𝘄𝘁𝗵 𝗔𝗰𝗰𝗲𝗹𝗲𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: The investment is expected to position RBL Bank for its next growth phase, unlocking new international opportunities, accelerating expansion in high-growth segments, & enhancing its ability to serve a more global customer base. 🌍
- 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝗶𝗰 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲: This marks a pivotal inflection point for RBL Bank, reinforcing confidence in its franchise & enabling expansion of cross-border corridors. It also signifies strengthening economic & financial linkages between India & the UAE. 🤝

𝗧𝗿𝗮𝗻𝘀𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗗𝗲𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗹𝘀:

- Emirates NBD will subscribe to up to 959,045,636 equity shares at ₹280 per share via a preferential issue, representing ~60% of RBL Bank’s post-issue share capital. 📈
- Emirates NBD's final shareholding is anticipated to be between 51% & 74%, subject to foreign ownership limits & the mandatory open offer.
- The transaction includes the eventual amalgamation of Emirates NBD’s India branch operations (Mumbai, Chennai, Gurugram) into RBL Bank, pending further regulatory clearances. 🏢
- Upon completion, Emirates NBD will become the promoter, & RBL Bank will operate as a foreign bank subsidiary per RBI framework. 🏦

Mr. Chandan Sinha, Chairman, RBL Bank, stated, “This approval reinforces confidence in our franchise and positions us to unlock new growth opportunities…”

Mr. R Subramaniakumar, MD & CEO, RBL Bank, added, “This milestone marks a transformational step forward… Our focus is on building a strong, agile, future-ready institution…” ✨

📊 RBL BANK LTD | 🏷️ Press Release / Media Release

🌐 Details: wegro.app/UWHn3r

RBL Bank Announces Receipt of All Regulatory & Governmental Approvals for Strategic Investment by Emirates NBD

Mumbai, 15 May 2026: RBL Bank Limited (RBL Bank) has achieved a major milestone by successfully completing the regulatory & governmental approval process for the proposed strategic investment by Emirates NBD. This significant development, first announced on October 18, 2025, marks a crucial step towards the transaction's finalization.

Key Highlights:

- Investment Amount: Emirates NBD will make a primary infusion of approximately USD 3 billion (~₹26,850 crore), one of the largest international investments in India's banking sector.
- Growth Acceleration: The investment is expected to position RBL Bank for its next growth phase, unlocking new international opportunities, accelerating expansion in high-growth segments, and enhancing its ability to serve a more global customer base.
- Strategic Importance: This marks a pivotal inflection point for RBL Bank, reinforcing confidence in its franchise and enabling expansion of cross-border corridors. It also signifies strengthening economic and financial linkages between India and the UAE.

Transaction Details:

- Emirates NBD will subscribe to up to 959,045,636 equity shares at ₹280 per share via a preferential issue, representing ~60% of RBL Bank’s post-issue share capital.
- Emirates NBD's final shareholding is anticipated to be between 51% and 74%, subject to foreign ownership limits and the mandatory open offer.
- The transaction includes the eventual amalgamation of Emirates NBD’s India branch operations (Mumbai, Chennai, Gurugram) into RBL Bank, pending further regulatory clearances.
- Upon completion, Emirates NBD will become the promoter, and RBL Bank will operate as a foreign bank subsidiary per RBI framework.

Mr. Chandan Sinha, Chairman, RBL Bank, stated, “This approval reinforces confidence in our franchise and positions us to unlock new growth opportunities…”

Mr. R Subramaniakumar, MD & CEO, RBL Bank, added, “This milestone marks a transformational step forward… Our focus is on building a strong, agile, future-ready institution…”

📊 Banking stocks may remain in focus following the major regulatory approval update.

post_highlights: $3 billion into RBL Bank (Emirates NBD acquiring up to 74%) The RBL deal is the one that matters. • It is the first-ever foreign majority acquisition of a profitable Indian bank approved by the RBI in April. • UAE is committing $5 billion to India's financial sector.

text:
UAE is committing $5 billion to India's financial sector. 

$3 billion into RBL Bank (Emirates NBD acquiring up to 74%), $1 billion into Sammaan Capital (IHC taking 43.5%), balance into infrastructure. PM Modi's Abu Dhabi visit today is the backdrop.

The RBL deal is the one that matters. First-ever foreign majority acquisition of a profitable Indian bank RBI approved in April. Emirates NBD's entry triggers a mandatory open offer for public shareholders. Tier-1 capital gets a $3 billion boost, cost of funds drops, and the bank gets a promoter with $43 billion market cap. The open offer price is the floor. Watch both stocks.

text:
We are thrilled to welcome Suvo Sarkar onboard as our Strategic Advisor. 🎉

A true legend of banking and digital transformation, Suvo brings 38+ years of banking experience across Asia and the Middle East - including 11 years at Emirates NBD, where he was Senior EVP & Group Head, Retail Banking and Wealth Management. At Emirates NBD, he co-led one of the region's most ambitious digital overhauls and founded Liv, the Middle East's first digital bank. He now leads 3D Advisory and serves as a senior advisor to McKinsey & Co and Mastercard.

We're building toward a future where banks don't just analyze customer data, they act on it, autonomously, in real time. Suvo has been on the other side of exactly this challenge, and his perspective will be invaluable as we build.

Beyond his stellar career, Suvo is a celebrated thought leader and storyteller, hosting the award-winning Money Majlis podcast and earning recognition as the Digital Leader of the Middle East by MIT Sloan Review. He brings not just expertise, but a genuine passion for the future of financial services.

Welcome, Suvo! We are honoured and energized to have your wisdom and vision with us on this journey.😊
